ogTitle: Kirup vs Kojaneerup South: Climate Safety

heading: Kirup vs Kojaneerup South — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kirup, Western Australia and Kojaneerup South, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Kirup has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Kojaneerup South (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kirup or Kojaneerup South safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kirup has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Kojaneerup South scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kirup scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Kojaneerup South scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kirup or Kojaneerup South?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Kirup has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
